  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

Simulation Benchmark Data Generation

Tanmay Tanna ttanna@ethz.ch

  • 1 1. Generate the manuscript-aligned simulation benchmark
  • 2 2. Cohort layout
  • 3 3. Injected technical effects
  • 4 4. Saved benchmark files

This notebook reproduces the shareable simulation data generation workflow described in the manuscript sections Simulation benchmark and Supplementary Methods S6. It creates the clean ground-truth matrix, adds metabolite-specific batch and drift effects, and writes the benchmark inputs used for downstream method comparisons.

1 1. Generate the manuscript-aligned simulation benchmark

source(file.path("..", "scripts", "simulate_metabolomics_benchmark.R"))

simulation <- simulate_metabolomics_benchmark(
  output_dir = file.path("..", "data", "simulated"),
  verbose = FALSE
)

meta <- simulation$metadata
design_table <- simulation$design_table
summary_values <- simulation$summary

2 2. Cohort layout

cohort_summary <- tibble::tibble(
  Metric = c(
    "Metabolites",
    "Total injections",
    "Plates",
    "Controls",
    "Study samples",
    "QC interval per plate"
  ),
  Value = c(
    summary_values$n_metabolites,
    summary_values$n_injections,
    summary_values$n_plates,
    summary_values$controls,
    summary_values$study_samples,
    summary_values$qc_interval
  )
)

knitr::kable(cohort_summary, align = c("l", "r"))
Metric Value
Metabolites 1000
Total injections 500
Plates 5
Controls 50
Study samples 450
QC interval per plate 10 
layout_plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  meta,
  ggplot2::aes(
    x = order_in_plate,
    y = factor(plate, levels = rev(unique(plate))),
    fill = sample_type
  )
) +
  ggplot2::geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.25) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = c(control = "#1f77b4", sample = "#d9d9d9")) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Simulation acquisition layout",
    subtitle = "Five plates of 100 injections with pooled controls inserted every 10 acquisitions.",
    x = "Order within plate",
    y = "Plate",
    fill = "Injection type"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(panel.grid = ggplot2::element_blank())

layout_plot

3 3. Injected technical effects

effect_summary <- tibble::tibble(
  Metric = c(
    "Metabolites with any batch effect",
    "Strong-shift subset among batch-affected metabolites",
    "Metabolites with drift on at least one plate",
    "Mean drift-active metabolite-plate pairs"
  ),
  Value = c(
    sprintf("%.1f%%", summary_values$batch_affected_pct),
    sprintf("%.1f%%", summary_values$strong_batch_subset_pct),
    sprintf("%.1f%%", 100 * mean(design_table$drift_effect_applied_any_plate)),
    sprintf("%.1f%%", summary_values$drift_active_pair_pct)
  )
)

knitr::kable(effect_summary, align = c("l", "r"))
Metric Value
Metabolites with any batch effect 71.6%
Strong-shift subset among batch-affected metabolites 8.9%
Metabolites with drift on at least one plate 59.7%
Mean drift-active metabolite-plate pairs 32.8% 
drift_type_counts <- design_table |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    linear = sum(drift_type_linear),
    nonlinear = sum(drift_type_nonlinear),
    ar1 = sum(drift_type_ar1),
    mixed = sum(drift_type_mixed)
  ) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(dplyr::everything(), names_to = "drift_type", values_to = "count")

drift_type_plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  drift_type_counts,
  ggplot2::aes(x = drift_type, y = count, fill = drift_type)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_col(width = 0.7, color = "#1f1f1f", linewidth = 0.25) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      linear = "#4e79a7",
      nonlinear = "#59a14f",
      ar1 = "#e15759",
      mixed = "#f28e2b"
    ),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Assigned drift morphologies across active metabolite-plate pairs",
    x = "Drift type",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

drift_type_plot

4 4. Saved benchmark files

saved_files <- tibble::tibble(
  File = basename(simulation$output_paths),
  Path = unname(simulation$output_paths)
)

knitr::kable(saved_files, align = c("l", "l"))
File Path
ground_truth.tsv ../data/simulated/ground_truth.tsv
shifted_profiles.tsv ../data/simulated/shifted_profiles.tsv
sample_metadata.tsv ../data/simulated/sample_metadata.tsv
simulation_design_table.tsv ../data/simulated/simulation_design_table.tsv

The written files provide the shareable synthetic benchmark used in the manuscript: a clean reference matrix, an artefact-bearing matrix, sample metadata, and a feature-level design audit table.
